title: Pronouns and Who Questions
objective: Use basic pronouns and ask who someone is using คือ and ใคร in short controlled exchanges.

teachingFrame.openingHook: You already know how to say hello and introduce your name. But how do you talk about other people? Today you learn the words for I, you, he, she — and how to ask who someone is.
teachingFrame.scenario: You are at a small gathering. Someone points to a person across the room. You ask who they are, get an answer, and confirm.
teachingFrame.learnerTakeaway: You can now say I, you, he/she, point to someone and say who they are, and ask who someone is.

sections.heading: I and you — the first pronouns
sections.purpose: Teach ฉัน, ผม, and คุณ as the building blocks for talking about people.
spokenNarration: Let's start with the most important words in any conversation — I and you.
In Thai, there are different words for I. The general one is ฉัน chǎn. If you are male and want to sound polite, use ผม phǒm instead.
For you, the safe polite word is คุณ khun. You already know this from คุณชื่ออะไร — what is your name. คุณ means you.
So if someone asks who you are, you can say ฉันชื่อ... chǎn chûue... — I am called... Or if you are male: ผมชื่อ... phǒm chûue...
drills: Listen and repeat: ฉัน (chǎn), ผม (phǒm), คุณ (khun). Say each one twice.
Pause-and-produce: I say a situation — male speaker or female speaker. You say the right word for I before I show the answer.
Response-building: I say คุณชื่ออะไร (khun chûue à-rai). You answer with ฉันชื่อ... or ผมชื่อ... and your name.

visualPlan.onScreenGoal: Show the three pronoun cards clearly with gender note on ผม.
teachingVisuals: Three pronoun cards: ฉัน, ผม, คุณ with transliteration and meaning
Gender indicator next to ผม showing male-polite usage
teacherCues: Point to each pronoun as you model it. Gesture to yourself for ฉัน/ผม, gesture to the learner for คุณ.

imageSupport.rationale: Pronouns are abstract — text cards with clear labels teach better than images.

sections.heading: He, she, and this — pointing to people
sections.purpose: Teach เขา, นี่, and คือ to build identity sentences.
spokenNarration: Now let's talk about other people. เขา khǎo means he or she. Thai uses the same word for both.
นี่ nîi means this. And คือ khuue means is. So when you put them together: นี่คือ... nîi khuue... means this is...
Try it: นี่คือไมค์ nîi khuue Mike — this is Mike. Or: เขาคือครูนาย khǎo khuue khruu Naai — he is Teacher Naai.
คือ is special — it connects a name or identity to a person. Think of it as the equals sign.
drills: Listen and repeat: เขา (khǎo), นี่ (nîi), คือ (khuue). Now together: นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
Substitution drill: I give a name. You say นี่คือ... with that name. Example: Mike → นี่คือไมค์.
Pause-and-produce: I point to someone and say เขาชื่ออะไร (khǎo chûue à-rai). You answer เขาชื่อ... with a name.

visualPlan.onScreenGoal: Show เขา, นี่, คือ as individual cards, then the chunk นี่คือ... as a combined card.
teachingVisuals: Three word cards: เขา, นี่, คือ
One chunk card: นี่คือ... with arrow showing how the parts combine
teacherCues: Point to a person or object when modelling นี่คือ... to make the demonstrative concrete.

imageSupport.rationale: Abstract grammar words — text cards are clearer than images.

sections.heading: Ask who someone is
sections.purpose: Teach ใคร and the chunk เขาคือใคร to form who-questions.
spokenNarration: Now the question word: ใคร khrai means who. In Thai, the question word stays where the answer would go.
So to ask who is he or she, you say: เขาคือใคร khǎo khuue khrai. Literally: he/she is who?
And to confirm: คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม khun khuue...châi mǎi — you are... right? You already know ใช่ from lesson 1.
Try the full exchange: เขาคือใคร — เขาคือไมค์ — ไมค์ใช่ไหม — ใช่ครับ.
onScreenBullets: เขาคือใคร | khǎo khuue khrai | who is he/she?
คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม | khun khuue...châi mǎi | you are...right?
drills: Listen and repeat: ใคร (khrai). Now the full question: เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
Response-building: I ask เขาคือใคร. You answer เขาคือ... with any name you choose.
Pause-and-produce: I say a name. You build the confirmation: คุณคือ[name]ใช่ไหม before I show it.

languageFocus.thai: คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม

visualPlan.onScreenGoal: Show ใคร as a standalone question word, then the two chunks as complete patterns.
teachingVisuals: Two chunk cards showing the full patterns with slots for names
teacherCues: Model the rising intonation on ใคร clearly. Point to an imaginary person when asking เขาคือใคร.

imageSupport.rationale: Question patterns — text-first teaching is clearest.

sections.heading: Put it together — a mini identity exchange
sections.purpose: Combine all new pronouns and patterns with review vocabulary in a realistic mini-dialogue.
spokenNarration: Let's use everything together. Imagine you are at a gathering and you see someone you don't know.
You turn to your friend and ask: เขาคือใคร khǎo khuue khrai — who is that?
Your friend answers: นี่คือแอน nîi khuue Anne. เขามาจากประเทศไทย khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet thai — she comes from Thailand.
You confirm: แอนใช่ไหม Anne châi mǎi — Anne, right? And your friend says: ใช่ครับ châi khráp — yes.
onScreenBullets: เขาคือใคร | khǎo khuue khrai | who is he/she?
นี่คือ... | nîi khuue... | this is...
เขามาจากประเทศไทย | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet thai | he/she comes from Thailand
drills: Role swap: I play the friend. You ask เขาคือใคร and I answer. Then you confirm with ใช่ไหม.
Substitution drill: I change the name and country. You rebuild the full exchange each time.
Production challenge: introduce yourself using ฉัน or ผม, then ask who the person next to you is.

visualPlan.onScreenGoal: Show a short exchange as a dialogue ladder with pronoun and identity patterns highlighted.
teachingVisuals: Dialogue ladder with 4 turns showing the full exchange
Highlighted slots where names and countries can be substituted
teacherCues: Model the full exchange first, then have the learner take turns speaking each role.

imageSupport.rationale: The dialogue ladder itself is the visual — no images needed.

pronunciationFocus.mouthMapAnchor: Focus on the rising tone contour: voice starts low and climbs. ใคร and ผม both rise.

roleplay.scenario: At a gathering, you ask your friend who someone is, learn their name and country, and confirm.

lines.speaker: You
lines.thai: ขอโทษครับ เขาคือใครครับ
lines.translit: khǎaw-thôot khráp khǎo khuue khrai khráp

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.thai: นี่คือแอนค่ะ

lines.speaker: You
lines.thai: เขามาจากประเทศอะไรครับ

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.thai: เขามาจากประเทศไทยค่ะ

lines.speaker: You
lines.thai: คนไทยใช่ไหมครับ

recap: How do you say I if you are male? ผม (phǒm).
How do you say this is...? นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
How do you ask who someone is? เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
How do you confirm someone's identity? คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม (khun khuue...châi mǎi).
What does คือ mean? It connects a person to their identity — is.
